title: The dual Ginzburg-Landau theory for a holographic superfluid/superconductor: Critical dynamics
abstract: Holographic superfluids/superconductors are one of the most studied systems in the AdS/CFT duality. In the low-energy, in the long-wavelength limit, they should be described by a Ginzburg-Landau theory. For critical dynamics, one expects that they belong to "model F" universality class. We consider a bulk 5-dimensional holographic superfluid/superconductor in the probe limit. For the holographic superconductor, we impose the boundary Maxwell equation to make the boundary Maxwell field dynamical. We identify the dual model F equations where numerical coefficients are obtained exactly.
summary: This paper derives the exact Ginzburg-Landau parameters for the critical dynamics of holographic superconductors. By matching bulk holographic calculations to the Model F universality class, the author determines precise numerical coefficients for the system's time-dependent behavior.
main_problem: The paper aims to identify the exact dual Ginzburg-Landau theory and the specific 'model F' universality class parameters for the critical dynamics of holographic superconductors and superfluids.
main_result: The author successfully identifies the dual Model F equations and obtains exact numerical coefficients for parameters such as relaxation time, diffusion constants, and thermodynamic susceptibility.
method: The study uses the AdS/CFT correspondence to solve bulk field equations in a 5D Schwarzschild-AdS black hole background using perturbation theory and the Martin-Siggia-Rose formalism.
model_or_system: A 5-dimensional Einstein-Maxwell-complex scalar system in the probe limit, which is holographically dual to a 4-dimensional system exhibiting superconductivity/superfluidity.
key_observables: Hydrodynamic poles (Maxwell diffusion and second sound modes), electric permittivity, magnetic permeability, conductivity, and the dynamic critical exponent z.
important_parameters: Chemical potential (mu), temperature (T), the deviation from criticality (epsilon_mu), and the large-Nc limit.
main_assumptions: The study assumes the probe limit (no backreaction on geometry), the large-Nc limit (suppressing fluctuations), and focuses on the low-energy, long-wavelength limit near the critical point.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the holographic model and the dual Ginzburg-Landau framework, proceeds to analyze the high-temperature phase dynamics, investigates the low-temperature phase and mode coupling, and concludes with the electromagnetic properties of the medium.
why_interesting: It provides an exact mapping of a holographic model to a known universality class (Model F), offering deep insights into the critical dynamics and transport coefficients of strongly coupled systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
